line: GROVE_RESULT: success - implemented multi-guard scope leak detection with violating file tracking and scope leak actions

event.summary: success - implemented multi-guard scope leak detection with violating file tracking and scope leak actions

line: - The guard-path feature was partially implemented already; the fastest path was fixing stale field/test drift and proving behavior with focused tests instead of reworking the CLI surface.

items: The guard-path feature was partially implemented already; the fastest path was fixing stale field/test drift and proving behavior with focused tests instead of reworking the CLI surface.

line: - Exact test names for binary and library crates required `cargo test -- --list` first; that avoided repeating filtered-out “0 tests” runs.

items: Exact test names for binary and library crates required `cargo test -- --list` first; that avoided repeating filtered-out “0 tests” runs.

line: - Kept scope-leak detection on the existing `AgentState::apply(AgentEvent::FileModified)` and classifier path rather than adding a parallel detector.

items: Kept scope-leak detection on the existing `AgentState::apply(AgentEvent::FileModified)` and classifier path rather than adding a parallel detector.

line: - Preserved `--guard` as clap `Vec<PathBuf>` with `value_delimiter=','`, which already supports comma-separated guards like `--guard src/auth/,tests/auth/`.

items: Preserved `--guard` as clap `Vec<PathBuf>` with `value_delimiter=','`, which already supports comma-separated guards like `--guard src/auth/,tests/auth/`.

line: - For multiple guards, report the most specific non-matching guard by longest shared prefix and deeper path length, so the emitted `guard_path` is more informative than always using the first guard.

items: For multiple guards, report the most specific non-matching guard by longest shared prefix and deeper path length, so the emitted `guard_path` is more informative than always using the first guard.

line: - Updated `saw-daemon::FileWatcher` to accept `Vec<PathBuf>` so watcher-side logging and tests align with multi-guard behavior.

items: Updated `saw-daemon::FileWatcher` to accept `Vec<PathBuf>` so watcher-side logging and tests align with multi-guard behavior.

line: - Fixed an unrelated compile blocker in `crates/saw-daemon/src/process_monitor.rs` (`&disk_usage`) so daemon tests could run.

items: Fixed an unrelated compile blocker in `crates/saw-daemon/src/process_monitor.rs` (`&disk_usage`) so daemon tests could run.

line: - `ScopeLeaking` still stores a single `guard_path` in the phase payload, so multi-guard reporting is “best matching guard”, not a full set of candidate guards.

items: `ScopeLeaking` still stores a single `guard_path` in the phase payload, so multi-guard reporting is “best matching guard”, not a full set of candidate guards.
